root_text.an5.1:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.an5.1:3.2: ‘saddhābalena samannāgatā bhavissāma sekhabalena, hirībalena samannāgatā bhavissāma sekhabalena, ottappabalena samannāgatā bhavissāma sekhabalena, vīriyabalena samannāgatā bhavissāma sekhabalena, paññābalena samannāgatā bhavissāma sekhabalenā’ti.
root_text.an5.1:3.5: Attamanā te bhikkhū bhagavato bhāsitaṁ abhinandunti.

translation_text.an5.1:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.an5.1:1.3: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.an5.1:2.1: “Mendicants, there are these five powers of a trainee.
translation_text.an5.1:2.3: The powers of faith, conscience, prudence, energy, and wisdom.
translation_text.an5.1:2.4: These are the five powers of a trainee.
translation_text.an5.1:3.1: So you should train like this:
translation_text.an5.1:3.2: ‘We will have the trainee’s powers of faith, conscience, prudence, energy, and wisdom.’
translation_text.an5.1:3.4: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.an5.1:3.5: Satisfied, the mendicants approved what the Buddha said.

variant_text.an5.1:2.3: hirībalaṁ → hiribalaṁ (bj, sya2ed, pts1ed) | vīriyabalaṁ → viriyabalaṁ (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed)

reference_text.an5.1:1.1: bj20.2, csp1ed16.1, csp2ed16.1, dr22.1, ms16A5_2, msdiv1, ndp17.273, pts-vp-pli3.1, vri37.1
